data.title: A whale trader sold 7,818 ETH at $3,714, resulting in a $3.47 million loss.
data.description: According to monitoring by on-chain analyst Ember, a whale who bought high and sold low sold 7,818 ETH at $3,714, resulting in a loss of $3.47 million. In half a month, this whale successfully turned 38.017 million DAI into $29.038 million through two waves of buying high and selling low, resulting in a loss of $8.979 million.

data.title: Orochi Network Completes $8 Million in New Funding, with Ethereum Foundation and Others Joining
data.description: Orochi Network, a decentralized data infrastructure project, announced the completion of a new round of financing of US$8 million, with participation from institutions such as the Ethereum Foundation. As of now, its total financing has reached US$20 million. The new funds will be used to build verifiable data infrastructure for areas such as RWA and stablecoins.

data.title: Cryptoquant: Short-term fluctuations do not necessarily mean there are structural problems in the market; long-term investors will enhance market resilience
data.description: Analysts at Cryptoquant say the market is facing shocks similar to those of 2021, but with a different structure. In previous cycles, panic phases saw increased reserves on exchanges, and the influx of liquidity on exchanges amplified selling pressure. Bitcoin balances on exchanges are now at a decade-low, indicating limited supply available for sale and a tighter market structure. With fewer Bitcoins held on exchanges, a sustained downward trend is unlikely. The behavior of long-term holders is also different. In 2020 and 2021, the long-term holder SOPR (LTH-SOPR) remained well below 1 for several months, indicating investors feared losses from the exits. However, during this decline, the ratio remained near neutral, indicating cautious profit-taking rather than fear-driven selling. Long-term investors continued to hold on during the volatility, strengthening market resilience. Looking back at past shocks, the March 2020 crash eliminated leverage, leading to significant accumulation by large investors and resulting in a V-shaped rebound. In May 2021, Bitcoin plummeted 30% due to the impact of Tesla and regulatory pressure. Large investors sold approximately 50,000 Bitcoins, subsequently repurchasing 34,000 at the bottom. In August 2023, a US debt downgrade triggered a 15% correction, briefly dipping the SOPR before quickly rebounding. Each of these events cleared out excessive leverage and ushered in a new round of accumulation. This downturn has made the market appear more mature. With dwindling reserves on trading platforms and a stable long-term holder base, temporary fluctuations do not equate to structural weaknesses, and Bitcoin is laying the foundation for its next upcycle.

data.title: Over the past hour, the entire network has experienced a liquidation of over $54 million, mainly due to short positions.
data.description: Coinglass data shows that in the past hour, the entire network had a liquidation of US$54.34141 million, of which short positions had a liquidation of US$50.2163 million and long positions had a liquidation of US$4.1278 million.

data.title: OKX to Launch ZBT (ZEROBASE) Pre-Market Trading
data.description: According to official news, OKX will launch pre-market trading of ZBT (ZEROBASE) at 7:30 PM (UTC+8) on October 17, 2025. ZEROBASE is a decentralized cryptographic infrastructure network that enables verifiable off-chain computations through zero-knowledge proofs and a trusted execution environment.

data.title: Tether Releases Open-Source Wallet Development Kit
data.description: According to Foresight News, Tether has announced the release of its open-source Wallet Development Kit (WDK). This new toolkit is designed to empower humans, machines, and AI agents to build, deploy, and use secure, multi-chain, self-custodial wallets. These wallets can be integrated into any device, ranging from the smallest embedded systems to mobile, desktop, or server operating systems.

data.title: Zcash price drops to $190 amid broader crypto pullback
data.description:
Zcash price dropped to the $190 support level. Macro headwinds also had Bitcoin falling to below $105,000 to trigger further bleeding across crypto. Analysts remain bullish despite the dip. Zcash (ZEC) tumbled to lows of $190, with its double-digit declines reflecting widespread market unease
source: https://coinjournal.net/news/zcash-price-drops-to-190-amid-broader-crypto-pullback/

data.title: Bitcoin Market Shows Resilience Amid Structural Changes
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Cryptoquant analysts have observed that the current market is facing challenges similar to those in 2021, but with a different structural composition. Unlike previous cycles where panic led to increased reserves on trading platforms and amplified selling pressure, the current Bitcoin balance on exchanges is at its lowest in a decade, indicating limited supply available for sale and a tighter market structure. The reduction in Bitcoin held by exchanges suggests that a sustained downward trend is unlikely.

Long-term holders are behaving differently compared to past periods. During 2020 and 2021, the Long-Term Holder Spent Output Profit Ratio (LTH-SOPR) remained below 1 for several months, indicating potential losses for investors fearing a market exit. However, during the recent downturn, this ratio has stayed near neutral, reflecting cautious profit-taking rather than fear-driven selling. Long-term investors continue to hold through volatility, enhancing market resilience.

Looking back at previous shocks, the March 2020 crash cleared leverage, leading to significant accumulation by large holders and a V-shaped recovery. In May 2021, Bitcoin fell 30% due to pressure from Tesla and regulatory actions, with large holders selling approximately 50,000 Bitcoins and later repurchasing 34,000 at the bottom. In August 2023, the U.S. debt rating downgrade triggered a 15% pullback, with SOPR briefly dropping before quickly rebounding. Each event cleared excessive leverage and initiated a new accumulation phase.

The current market downturn appears more mature, with reduced exchange reserves and stable long-term holders. Temporary volatility does not equate to structural weakness, as Bitcoin is laying the groundwork for the next upward cycle.

data.title: Trump Indicates Possible Shift in Tariff Policy Towards China
data.description: According to Foresight News, U.S. President Donald Trump has suggested a potential change in the tariff policy towards China. When asked about maintaining high tariffs on Chinese goods, Trump responded that he would not continue with the current approach.
